rationale: The cited paper is about the attraction effect’s robustness across many decoy tests, while the citing paper studies serotonin’s impact on choice deferral and the compromise effect, so topical overlap appears indirect (both are context-effects literature). The only matched evidence is a DOI-level reference with no in-text citation context or quoted usage, so there is not enough evidence to confidently classify it as purely incidental or clearly substantive.
